com.mentor.chs.api.query

Interface IXQuery


  • public interface IXQuery
    IXQuery interface is used to fetch extensibility objects of a particular type by providing its name, its extensibility class and a filter condition (IXFilterExpression). In addition, queries work on a scope that help isolate the data to be fetched by providing a scope, in the form of a related query. For example one can provide a query for particular project as a related query to a query for designs. When such a query is executed only designs of that particular project will be returned by the design query. Query for projects will not require a scope (i.e no related queries need be provided). All others require atleast one scoping query. Relational framework looksup how one entity is related to another, this is most often predefined. For example wires are related to connectors through connections from their pins, so a query for wires with related query for connector will fetch wires attached to the connectors only. Relations are documented in elsewhere in custom report development. Note: Multiple related queries will result in a union of results from each query.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addRelatedQuery(IXQuery relatedQuery)
      A related query is expected to return objects that are related to entity this query is expected to fetch.
      java.lang.String getCofClassName()
      Each query in the reports can have an additional filter on the cof class name.
      java.lang.Class<? extends IXObject> getEntityClass()
      get class of the entity in this query.
      java.lang.String getEntityName() 
      IXFilterExpression getFilterExpression() 
      java.util.Collection<IXQuery> getRelatedQueries()
      get all queries that form the scope for this query.
      java.util.Collection<IXObject> getResult()
      This function executes the query to return a list of entities which satisfy the filter expression for the defined scope.

    • Method Detail

      • getEntityName

        java.lang.String getEntityName()
        Returns:
        unique html name of the entity ("Wire/Net/Device" etc) that defines this query.

      • getEntityClass

        java.lang.Class<? extends IXObject> getEntityClass()
        get class of the entity in this query.
        Returns:
        class

      • getFilterExpression

        IXFilterExpression getFilterExpression()
        Returns:
        filter expression use to filter objects queried on.

      • getRelatedQueries

        java.util.Collection<IXQuery> getRelatedQueries()
        get all queries that form the scope for this query. Result of this query will only result in objects related to those from related-queries.
        Returns:
        collection of related queries that form the scope of this query

        also see IXQuery:removeRelatedQuery and IXQuery:addRelatedQuery

      • getResult

        java.util.Collection<IXObject> getResult()
        This function executes the query to return a list of entities which satisfy the filter expression for the defined scope.
        Returns:
        resulSet

      • addRelatedQuery

        void addRelatedQuery(IXQuery relatedQuery)
        A related query is expected to return objects that are related to entity this query is expected to fetch. This helps isolate objects that are returned by this query to those that are related to objects returned by the related query This function may be used to add related queries for this query.
        Parameters:
        relatedQuery - relatedQuery implementing IXQuery.

      • getCofClassName

        java.lang.String getCofClassName()
        Each query in the reports can have an additional filter on the cof class name.
        Returns:
        the name of the cof class assosciated with the XObject on whcih filtering has to be done.
